Name: File System - Combine all files in directory into single file
Description:
This step template will take all the files in a single directory, sort them alphabetically, and combine them into a single file.  

Optional, it will create an artifact for that file so it can be reviewed.

Parameters.Name: CombineFiles.Directory.PackageSource
Parameters.Label: Source Package Step
Parameters.HelpText: **Optional** - The name of the package step containing the files to combine.  Leave this blank if the full path is in the Source Directory.

Parameters.Name: CombineFiles.Directory.Source
Parameters.Label: Source Directory
Parameters.HelpText: **Optional** - The folder containing all the files to combine into a single file.  If using this with the source package step variable then this is the relative path to that.  Otherwise this is the full path to it.

Parameters.Name: CombineFiles.Directory.Filter
Parameters.Label: Source File Filter
Parameters.HelpText: **Optional** - The filter to apply when looking for tiles.  Example *.sql or *.txt.  If omitted it will grab all files in the directory.

Parameters.Name: CombineFiles.Destination.FileName
Parameters.Label: Combined File Name
Parameters.HelpText: **Required** - the file name of all the combined files.  Must include the full path.  IE C:\Testing\NewFile.sql

Parameters.Name: CombineFiles.Destination.CommentCharacters
Parameters.Label: Comment Characters
Parameters.HelpText: **Optional** - Use this if you want to include the file name in your combined file as comments.  You will need to specify the comment characters for the language of the file.  For example "--" for SQL files, "//" for C# files.

Parameters.Name: CombineFiles.Destination.CreateArtifact
Parameters.Label: Create Artifact
Parameters.HelpText: Indicates if the combined file should be uploaded to Octopus Deploy as an artifact.  Useful when combining multiple SQL scripts into a single file so a DBA can review it.
